description: Classic nursery rhyme style with simple repetitive melody and traditional sing-song rhythm
suno_tags: nursery rhyme, gentle female vocals, acoustic guitar, tambourine, simple melody, warm, singalong, 90 BPM
vocal_style: Warm gentle adult female voice, clear and lilting, traditional sing-song phrasing with natural rhythm

tips: Use the classic nursery rhyme sing-song cadence
Strong AABB couplet rhymes — every two lines rhyme
Strophic form works well — same melody, new words each verse
Use character names and simple story arcs

example_lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Little Rosie had a hat
Red and round and fancy that
She wore it to the park one day
And all her friends came out to play

[Verse 2]
They played with balls and jumped the rope
Slid down the slide on the grassy slope
Little Rosie laughed with glee
Happy as a child can be

[Chorus]
Round and round the garden go
Singing high and singing low
Clap your hands and stomp your feet
Nursery rhymes are oh so sweet!

[Verse 3]
When the sun began to set
Rosie said, "Don't leave me yet!"
One more game before we're done
One more song, just one more one

[Verse 4]
So they sang beneath the tree
All together, you and me
When the moon came up so bright
Rosie whispered, "What a night!"

[Chorus]
Round and round the garden go
Singing high and singing low
Clap your hands and stomp your feet
Nursery rhymes are oh so sweet!

[Outro]
Goodnight, Rosie, goodnight all!
[End]
do: Use classic couplet rhyme (AABB) for that traditional feel
Create a simple character or scenario in the opening verse
Include physical actions kids can mimic (clap, stomp, jump)
Keep the melody implied through consistent syllable patterns
Tell a tiny complete story across the verses
dont: Never use children's choir or kids singing in suno_tags
Avoid complex story arcs — keep the narrative simple and linear
Don't use modern slang or references
Avoid dark or violent traditional nursery rhyme themes
Don't make it longer than 2 minutes — short and sweet
